title: Additively manufactured articles with lattice supports and methods and systems for production thereof
abstract: Articles with lattice supports capable of chemical removal, and methods and systems for production thereof are provided. The method comprises forming a part in a powder bed deposition region of an additive manufacturing apparatus from a powder. An open-cell lattice, comprising an unbound region and a bound region is formed in communication with at least a portion of the part. The open-cell lattice is adapted to restrain the part in the powder bed deposition region. The unbound region is adapted to form a void capable of receiving an etchant.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method comprising: forming a part in a powder bed deposition region of an additive manufacturing apparatus from a powder; forming an open-cell lattice in communication with at least a portion of the part, the lattice adapted to restrain the part in the powder bed deposition region, the lattice comprising an unbound region and a bound region, the unbound region adapted to form a void capable of receiving an etchant. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the lattice adapted to restrain the part in the powder bed deposition region comprises the lattice adapted to support and to prevent movement of the part in the powder bed deposition region. 3. The method of any one of claims 1-2, further comprising: forming a frame in communication with the lattice, the frame adapted to restrain the lattice. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the frame comprises the same density as the part. 5. The method of any one of claims 1-4, further comprising: removing powder from the unbound region from the lattice structure to form the void. 6. The method of any one of claims 1-5, further comprising: chemically degrading the lattice. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein chemically degrading the lattice comprises passing an etchant through the void. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein passing the etchant through the void comprises at least one of pumping the etchant into the void and submerging the part and lattice in the etchant. 9. The method of any one of claims 7-8, wherein the etchant is at least one compound selected from the group consisting of a mineral acid, an organic acid, ferric chloride, an alkali hydroxide, an ammonium hydroxide, an organic hydroxide, an alkali metal carbonate, and alkali metal bicarbonate, alkali metal hypochlorite, bromine, iodine, an alcohol, sodium stannate, potassium stannate, sodium gallate, potassium gallate, sodium zincate, potassium zincate, a mercury salt, a gallium salt, metallic mercury, metallic gallium, and an amine. 10. The method of any one of claims 5-9, wherein removal of the lattice exposes an exterior surface of the part. 11. The method of any one of claims 1-10, wherein the lattice comprises at least one structure selected from the group consisting of a diamond lattice, a tetrahedral lattice, a dodecahedron lattice, an octet-truss lattice, a cubic lattice, a tetrakaidecahedron lattice, a truncated cuboctahedron lattice, a gyroid lattice, a helical lattice, a voronoi patterned lattice, a hexagonal lattice, and a Schwartz lattice. 12. The method of any one of claims 1-11, wherein the lattice comprises an interior open volume in the range of 20% to 90%. 13. The method of any one of claims 1-12, wherein the lattice comprises a pore size in the range of 100 pm to 5 mm. 14. The method of any one of claims 1-13, wherein the lattice comprises a strut size in the range of 100 pm to 2 mm. 15. The method of any one of claims 1-14, wherein the lattice contacts an exterior surface of the part. 16. The method of any one of claims 1-15, wherein the powder comprises at least one of metallic particles, polymeric particles, and ceramic particles. 17. The method of any one of claims 1-16, wherein the powder comprises at least one material selected from the group consisting of titanium particles, titanium alloy particles, aluminum particles, aluminum alloy particles, nickel particles, nickel alloy particles, iron particles, iron alloy particles, cobalt particles, cobalt alloy particles, copper particles, copper alloy particles, molybdenum particles, molybdenum alloy 

 particles, magnesium particles, magnesium alloy particles, tantalum particles, tantalum alloy particles, chromium particles, chromium alloy particles, tungsten particles, tungsten alloy particles, zinc particles, zinc alloy particles, silver particles, silver alloy particles, chromium particles, chromium alloy particles, tin particles, tin alloy particles, gold particles, gold alloy particles, platinum particles, platinum alloy particles, zirconium particles, and zirconium alloy particles.. 18. The method of any one of claims 1-17, wherein the powder comprises a median particle size in a range of 50 nm to 325 pm. 19. The method of any one of claims 1-18, wherein the part is configured as at least one of an aerospace component, an automotive component, a transportation component, and a building and construction component. 20. An article produced by an additive manufacturing apparatus, the article comprising: an additively manufactured part; and an open-cell lattice in communication with at least a portion of the part, the lattice adapted to restrain the part, the lattice comprising an unbound region and a bound region, the unbound region adapted to form a void capable of receiving an etchant. 21. The article of claim 20, wherein the lattice adapted to restrain the part comprises the lattice adapted to support and to prevent movement of the part. 22. The article of any one of claims 20-21, further comprising: a frame in communication with the lattice, the frame adapted to restrain the lattice. 23. The article of claim 22, wherein the frame comprises the same density as the part. 24. The article of any one of claims 22-23, wherein the frame and the part comprise the same material. 25. The article of any one of claims 20-24, wherein the lattice and the part comprise the same material. 26. The article of any one of claims 20-25, further comprising 

 a void in the lattice, wherein the void is formed by removal of powder from the unbound region. 27. The article of any one of claims 20-26, wherein the lattice comprises at least one structure selected from the group consisting of a diamond lattice, a tetrahedral lattice, a dodecahedron lattice, an octet-truss lattice, a cubic lattice, a tetrakaidecahedron lattice, a truncated cuboctahedron lattice, a gyroid lattice, a helical lattice, a voronoi patterned lattice, a hexagonal lattice, and a Schwartz lattice. 28. The article of any one of claims 20-27, wherein the lattice comprises an interior open volume in the range of 20% to 90%. 29. The article of any one of claims 20-28, wherein the lattice comprises a pore size in the range of 100 pm to 5 mm. 30. The article of any one of claims 20-29, wherein the lattice comprises a strut size in the range of 100 pm to 2 mm. 31. The article of any one of claims 20-30, wherein the lattice contacts an exterior surface of the part. 32. The article of any one of claims 20-31, wherein the lattice has a composition adapted to be chemically degraded by an etchant to expose an exterior surface of the part. 33. The article of any one of claims 20-32, wherein the lattice comprises at least one of a uniform thickness adapted to be chemically degraded prior to the etchant chemically degrading the part and a non-uniform thickness. 34. The article of any one of claims 20-33, wherein the lattice has a microstructure adapted to be chemically degraded by an etchant to expose an exterior surface of the part. 35. The article of any one of claims 20-34, wherein the part comprises at least one of metallic material, polymeric material, and ceramic material. 36. The article of any one of claims 20-35, wherein the part comprises at least one material selected from the group consisting of titanium, titanium alloy, aluminum, aluminum alloy, nickel, nickel alloy, iron particles, iron alloy, cobalt, cobalt alloy, 

 copper, copper alloy, molybdenum, molybdenum alloy, magnesium, magnesium alloy, tantalum, tantalum alloy, chromium, chromium alloy, tungsten, tungsten alloy, zinc, zinc alloy, silver, silver alloy, chromium, chromium alloy, tin, tin alloy, gold, gold alloy, platinum, platinum alloy, zirconium, and zirconium alloy. 37. The article of any one of claims 20-36, wherein the part is configured as at least one of an aerospace component, an automotive component, a transportation component, and a building and construction component. 38. A powder bed additive manufacturing system comprising: a powder bed deposition region adapted to receive layers of powder and comprising a powder bed deposition surface; a powder deposition module adapted to dispose layers of powder in the powder bed deposition region; a joining module adapted to process powder disposed in the powder bed deposition region to thereby form a part and an open-cell lattice in communication with at least a portion of the part from the powder, the lattice adapted to restrain the part in the powder bed deposition region, the lattice comprising an unbound region and a bound region, the unbound region adapted to form a void capable of receiving an etchant; and a removal module adapted to provide an etchant to the void. 39. The system of claim 38, wherein the joining module comprises at least one of a binder deposition module, a laser module, an electron beam module, and a plasma torch. 40. The system of any one of claims 38-39, wherein the powder deposition module comprises at least one of a blade, a roller, a brush, and a hopper. 41. The system of any one of claims 38-40, wherein the removal module comprises at least one of a vessel and a pump, the vessel adapted to receive the etchant and to submerge the part, and the pump adapted to pass the etchant through the void. 42. The system of any one of claims 38-41, wherein the joining module is adapted to form a frame in communication with the lattice, the frame adapted to restrain the lattice. 43. The system of any one of claims 38-42, further comprising a void formation module adapted to remove powder from the unbound region from the lattice structure to form the void. 44. The system of any one of claims 38-43, wherein the etchant is at least one compound selected form the group consisting of a mineral acid, an organic acid, ferric chloride, an alkali hydroxide, an ammonium hydroxide, an organic hydroxide, an alkali metal carbonate, and alkali metal bicarbonate, alkali metal hypochlorite, bromine, iodine, an alcohol, sodium stannate, potassium stannate, sodium gallate, potassium gallate, sodium zincate, potassium zincate, a mercury salt, a gallium salt, metallic mercury, metallic gallium, and an amine. 45. The system of any one of claims 38-44, wherein the lattice comprises at least one structure selected form the group consisting of a diamond lattice, a tetrahedral lattice, a dodecahedron lattice, an octet-truss lattice, a cubic lattice, a tetrakaidecahedron lattice, a truncated cuboctahedron lattice, a gyroid lattice, a helical lattice, a voronoi patterned lattice, a hexagonal lattice, and a Schwartz lattice. 46. The system of any one of claims 38-45, wherein the lattice comprises an interior open volume in the range of 20% to 90%. 47. The system of any one of claims 38-46, wherein the lattice comprises a pore size in the range of 100 pm to 5 mm. 48. The system of any one of claims 38-47, wherein the lattice comprises a strut size in the range of 100 pm to 2 mm. 49. The system of any one of claims 38-48, wherein the lattice contacts an exterior surface of the part. 50. The system of any one of claims 38-49, wherein the lattice comprises at least one of a uniform thickness adapted to be chemically degraded prior to the etchant chemically degrading the part and a non-uniform thickness. 51. The system of any one of claims 38-50, wherein the powder comprises at least one of metallic particles, polymeric particles, and ceramic particles. 52. The system of any one of claims 38-51, wherein the part is configured as at least one of an aerospace component, an automotive component, a transportation component, and a building and construction component. 53. The system of any one of claims 38-52, wherein the system is adapted to conduct at least one additive manufacturing process selected from binder jet additive manufacturing, electron beam melting, direct metal laser sintering, selective laser melting, and selective laser sintering.
